title: Cooch Behar Palace
tagline: Cooch Behar Palace — heritage city of West Bengal

overview.short: Cooch Behar Palace is a landmark in Cooch Behar city, West Bengal.
overview.description: Cooch Behar Palace is a landmark in Cooch Behar city, West Bengal. It was designed in the Italian Renaissance architecture style and was built in 1887, during the reign of Maharaja Nripendra Narayan of the Koch dynasty. It is currently a museum.

topPlaces.name: Cooch Behar Stadium
topPlaces.description: Cooch Behar Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium in Cooch Behar, Bengal. The ground is mainly used for organizing matches of football, cricket and other sports.

topPlaces.name: Takagachh
topPlaces.description: Takagachh is a census town in the Cooch Behar II CD block in the Cooch Behar Sadar subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Suniti Academy
topPlaces.description: Sunity Academy is a higher secondary girls' school in Cooch Behar, West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Madan Mohan temple, Cooch Behar
topPlaces.description: Madan Mohan temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Krishna, who is worshipped in the form of Madan Mohan in this temple. Maa Kali, Maa Tara and Maa Bhavani are the other major Hindu deities who are also worshipped in the temple.

topPlaces.name: Cooch Behar
topPlaces.description: Cooch Behar (Bengali pronunciation: [kot͡ʃ biɦar]), also known as Koch Bihar, is a city in the Indian state of West Bengal and it stands on bank of the Torsa river. The city is the headquarters of the Cooch Behar district.

topPlaces.name: Baisguri
topPlaces.description: Baisguri is a census town in the Cooch Behar II CD block in the Cooch Behar Sadar subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Chakchaka
topPlaces.description: Chakchaka is a census town and gram panchayat in the Cooch Behar II CD block in the Cooch Behar Sadar subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Gudam Maharaniganj
topPlaces.description: Gudam Maharaniganj is a village in the Cooch Behar I CD block in the Cooch Behar Sadar subdivision of the Cooch Behar district in West Bengal, India

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cooch Behar Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cooch Behar Palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cooch Behar Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bagdogra Airport (Siliguri/Darjeeling) (117 km) and the nearest railway station is Alipurduar Junction (20 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cooch Behar Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cooch Behar Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cooch Behar Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Cooch Behar Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cooch Behar Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Cooch Behar Stadium, Takagachh, Suniti Academy, Madan Mohan temple, Cooch Behar, Cooch Behar.
